summary: British playwright known for 'comedies of menace' including The Dumb Waiter, The Birthday Party, The Homecoming, Betrayal, The Caretaker, and Old Times. Nobel laureate (2005). Also a screenwriter.

works.name: The Dumb Waiter
works.description: Two hitmen, Ben and Gus, wait in a basement for their assignment. They argue about the phrase 'light the kettle' vs. 'put on the kettle.' A speaking tube (the dumb waiter) delivers mysterious restaurant orders like 'Ormitha Macarounada.' Gus reads news about a child killing a cat and a man run over by a lorry. Wilson sends orders through the device. At the end, Ben is hired to kill his partner Gus, who returns stripped of clothing and his gun.

clues.clue: Hitmen Ben and Gus receive orders through the title device
examples: the mysterious Wilson sends orders through the title device to hitmen Ben and Gus

clues.clue: Argue about 'light the kettle' vs. 'put on the kettle'
examples: argue about the difference between 'light the kettle' and 'put on the kettle'

clues.clue: Ben is hired to kill Gus; Gus returns stripped of clothing and gun
examples: Ben is hired to kill his partner Gus

clues.clue: Gus reads news about child killing a cat / man under a lorry
examples: a 'child of eight' killing a cat and a 'man of eighty-seven' crawling under a lorry

works.name: The Birthday Party
works.description: Stanley, who pretends to be a concert pianist, lives at Meg's boarding house. Meg gives Stanley a toy drum. Goldberg and McCann arrive and terrorize Stanley. A game of blind man's bluff climaxes the play — Stanley's glasses are broken and he attempts to rape Lulu. Two men take Stanley away. Meg opens the play serving cornflakes and ends saying 'I was the belle of the ball.'

clues.clue: Goldberg and McCann terrorize Stanley at Meg's boarding house
examples: Goldberg and McCann terrorize Stanley

clues.clue: Meg gives Stanley a toy drum; blind man's bluff game
examples: Meg gives Stanley a toy drum

clues.clue: Stanley's glasses broken; attempts to rape Lulu during blind man's bluff
examples: gets his glasses broken and attempts to rape Lulu

clues.clue: Meg says 'I was the belle of the ball' at the end
examples: 'I was the belle of the ball'

works.name: The Homecoming
works.description: Philosopher Teddy brings his wife Ruth to his father's family home in London. Ruth refuses to 'go the whole hog' in a sexual encounter with Joey. Ruth ultimately stays to work as a prostitute for the family while Teddy returns to America. Chauffeur Sam reveals Jessie's affair before collapsing. Lenny argues about his cheese roll being eaten. Pinter's first wife Vivien Merchant starred in the 1973 adaptation.

clues.clue: Ruth stays to work as a prostitute; Teddy returns to America
examples: Ruth, the only female character, abandons her husband Teddy to work as a prostitute

clues.clue: Joey doesn't 'go the whole hog' during a two-hour sexual encounter
examples: a woman refuses to 'go the whole hog'

clues.clue: Sam reveals Jessie's affair before collapsing
examples: a chauffeur reveals Jessie's affair before collapsing

works.name: Betrayal
works.description: Told in reverse chronological order. Jerry has an affair with his friend Robert's wife Emma. Robert reveals he was aware of the affair. The unseen author Casey is frequently mentioned. Jerry and Robert run a publishing company and supposedly play squash (but never do).

clues.clue: Told in reverse chronological order — Jerry's affair with Emma
examples: Robert reveals he was aware of Jerry's affair with his wife Emma in a play... in reverse chronological order

clues.clue: The unseen author Casey; characters play squash (but never do)
examples: never play squash together despite repeatedly mentioning it

clues.clue: Jerry tossed a child in the air at a party; doesn't remember it was his kitchen
examples: tossing another character's child in the air at a party

works.name: The Caretaker
works.description: Aston invites the homeless Davies into his flat and offers him the title job. Mick (Aston's brother) smashes a statue of the Buddha while arguing with Davies. Davies never goes to Sidcup to recover his papers. Michael Billington argued Sidcup acts as a 'Kentish Eldorado.'

clues.clue: Mick smashes a Buddha statue; Davies never goes to Sidcup
examples: Mick smashes a statue of the Buddha while arguing with Davies

clues.clue: Aston invites homeless Davies and offers him the title job
examples: Aston inviting the homeless Davies into his flat

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: British playwright. 'Comedies of menace' — term coined by Irving Wardle. Nobel Prize lecture 'Art, Truth & Politics' (2005) denounced U.S. invasion of Iraq. Visited imprisoned authors in Turkey with Arthur Miller. Close friend of Sarah Kane (said 'she was a poet' at her funeral). Directed a revival of Coward's Blithe Spirit. Adapted Proust's In Search of Lost Time (unproduced). Screenwriter — collaborated with Joseph Losey on The Servant; adapted The Comfort of Strangers. Also wrote Old Times, A Kind of Alaska, No Man's Land, One for the Road, Ashes to Ashes, and the sketch Applicant.

clues.clue: 'Comedies of menace' — coined by Irving Wardle
examples: 'comedies of menace'

clues.clue: Nobel lecture 'Art, Truth & Politics' denounced Iraq invasion
examples: 'Art, Truth & Politics,' his 2005 Nobel Lecture

clues.clue: Close friend of Sarah Kane — said 'she was a poet' at funeral
examples: this close friend of Kane only said, 'she was a poet'

clues.clue: Collaborated with Joseph Losey on The Servant
examples: collaborated with the director Joseph Losey

comprehensive_summary:
Harold Pinter (1930-2008) was a British playwright, screenwriter, and Nobel laureate known for his 'comedies of menace,' a term coined by Irving Wardle. His plays combine absurdist humor with an atmosphere of undefined threat.

The Dumb Waiter is his most frequently clued play, featuring the hitmen Ben and Gus who wait in a basement, argue about 'light the kettle' vs. 'put on the kettle,' and receive mysterious restaurant orders through the title device. At the end, Ben is hired to kill Gus, who returns stripped of clothing and his gun.

The Birthday Party features Goldberg and McCann terrorizing Stanley at Meg's boarding house. Meg gives Stanley a toy drum, and the play climaxes with a blind man's bluff game. Betrayal unfolds in reverse chronological order, revealing Jerry's affair with his friend Robert's wife Emma. The Homecoming sees philosopher Teddy bring his wife Ruth home, where she ultimately stays to work as a prostitute while Teddy returns to America. The Caretaker features Mick smashing a Buddha statue while arguing with the homeless Davies.

Pinter's Nobel lecture 'Art, Truth & Politics' denounced the Iraq invasion. He was a close friend of Sarah Kane and collaborated with Joseph Losey on screenplays.

cards.front: Play: hitmen Ben and Gus argue about 'light the kettle'; receive restaurant orders through the title device; Ben is hired to kill Gus
Play: Goldberg and McCann terrorize Stanley at Meg's boarding house; blind man's bluff game; Meg gives Stanley a toy drum
Play: told in reverse chronological order; Jerry's affair with his friend Robert's wife Emma
Play: Ruth stays to work as a prostitute while Teddy returns to America; Joey doesn't 'go the whole hog'
Play: Mick smashes a Buddha statue; homeless Davies never goes to Sidcup to recover his papers
Playwright: 'comedies of menace' term coined by Irving Wardle; Nobel lecture 'Art, Truth & Politics' denounced Iraq invasion
